ocr.text: The electricity generation mix in the Asia-Pacific region is undergoing a significant transformation as countries strive to meet
their energy needs while addressing environmental concerns. As depicted in the accompanying data table, the financial metrics
associated with various renewable energy sources highlight emerging trends and market dynamics. Notably, wind energy is
projected to account for a substantial 25% of the generation mix by 2025, reflecting a robust growth trajectory in line with
global sustainability goals.
ElectricityGenerationMix  TotalGeneration
RenewableSources  FossilFuels  Nuclear  MarketOverview

Hydro  2000  5  15  15  [HIDDEN]
Biomass  800  10  5  3  2
Geothermal  300  12  [HIDDEN]  1  1
NaturalGas  5000  -5  30  20  15
TotalGeneration  18800  6  100  100  100
This shift is further underscored by wind's current market share of 20%, illustrating its increasing significance within the
region's energy landscape. Meanwhile, solar energy is anticipated to maintain its leadership position with a forecasted share of
50% in 2025, despite a slight decline in the 2024 actuals, which stood at 60%. Other renewable sources, such as hydroelectric
power, will also play an integral role, with the 2024 actual generation amounting to 10 AUD million. Geothermal energy,
although currently contributing a modest 3% to the market share, offers potential for growth as technological advancements
reduce costs and enhance efficiency. The year-on-year changes reflected in these metrics indicate a competitive and evolving
sector, where investment and innovation are crucial for meeting future energy demands and achieving sustainability targets. The
following table provides a detailed overview of the electricity generation mix, including the amount in AUD million and
projections for the coming years, illustrating the diverse pathways toward a cleaner energy future.
